title: echinaria capitata var. pumila
intro: Echinaria capitata var. pumila, also known as Dwarf Burdock or Small Burdock, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small, daisy-like flowers and hairy leaves. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its rich content of inulin, a type of dietary fiber, which has been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, Echinaria capitata var. pumila is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in dried and powdered forms, to support digestive health and overall well-being.
